diagnosis.score_summary: Overall alignment is strong at 77/100, with perfect consistency in tactical execution like Visuals, Messaging, and Archetypes. However, critical gaps in strategic pillars—Focus, Mission, and Functional Benefits—reveal a brand with two different brains, telling two different stories about its fundamental purpose and value.

truth_1.headline: Selling on Site, Schooling on Amazon
truth_1.gap_description: Your website is a 'Hard Product Sell,' focused on conversion and the pitch. Your Amazon PDP is 'Science Education,' focused on building trust with a thesis of proof points. You are treating customers like buyers on one channel and students on the other.
truth_1.evidence: [FOCUS] WWW: 'Hard Product Sell' — '"Goes well with" cross-sell section.' vs. PDP: 'Science Education' — 'PETA APPROVED Vegan...98% BIODEGRADABLE FORMULA...no sulfates, no parabens'
truth_1.why_matters: This schizophrenia confuses the customer's intent. A customer ready to buy is forced into a lecture on Amazon, while a customer trying to research on your site gets a hard sell. This friction slows down the path to purchase on both channels.
truth_1.the_hard_choice: You are running two different business models: a fast-moving sales counter and a university library. The conflict is slowing both down. Option A: Make Amazon a pure sales tool, stripping the educational content to mirror the website's directness. Option B: Acknowledge Amazon is a research hub and rebuild your website to be a richer, thesis-driven educational platform. A sales pitch works for a day, but an education builds trust for a decade.

truth_2.headline: Saving The World vs. Saving Your Skin
truth_2.gap_description: Your website claims a world-changing mission as 'The Earth Saviors' aiming to 'redefine beauty standards' for all. Your Amazon page has a much smaller ambition as 'The Democratizers,' focused on providing an accessible, effective product for an individual's skin.
truth_2.evidence: [MISSION & VISION] WWW: 'The Earth Saviors' — 'We want to redefine beauty standards' vs. PDP: 'The Democratizers' — 'Safe for all skin types - even you - dry skin, have nothing to worry about'
truth_2.why_matters: This disconnect dilutes your brand's purpose. Customers who believe in the grand mission will see the Amazon page as shallow. Customers who just want a good product will see the website's mission as irrelevant and inauthentic. You lose credibility with both audiences.
truth_2.the_hard_choice: Your brand's soul is split between being a social movement and being a great soap. You must choose one identity. Option A: Commit to being the best, most accessible product on the shelf and remove the world-saving mission from your website. Option B: Infuse your Amazon presence with the 'Self-Esteem Project' story to prove your grand mission is more than just a slogan. A brand can be a protest or a product, but when it tries to be both at once, it becomes a platitude.

truth_3.headline: The Fixer vs. The Shield
truth_3.gap_description: There is a fundamental conflict in the product's core job. The website sells it as an agent of 'Restoration' that actively fixes a problem ('eliminates bacteria'). The Amazon page sells it as an agent of 'Protection' that prevents future problems ('protects skin from dryness').
truth_3.evidence: [FUNCTIONAL BENEFITS] WWW: 'Restoration' — 'The Fixer (Reverses damage)' vs. PDP: 'Protection' — 'The Shield (Defense)'
truth_3.why_matters: A customer doesn't know if they are buying a powerful treatment or a gentle preventative. This confusion impacts the perceived strength of the product, the reason for purchase, and satisfaction after use. You are selling two different solutions.
truth_3.the_hard_choice: Is your product a sword or a shield? Right now, you claim it's both, which is confusing. Option A: Commit to the 'Shield' narrative of gentle, daily protection, which aligns with the master brand, and soften the aggressive 'Fixer' language on the website. Option B: Double down on the 'Restoration' story, positioning this as a high-strength, problem-solving product and update Amazon to match. A shield is bought for peace of mind; a sword is bought to solve a problem. You are selling two different emotions.

truth_4.headline: The Authority Is Drifting
truth_4.gap_description: Both channels align on the purpose of being 'The Guardian,' which is a strength. However, the foundation of this authority is subtly drifting. The website relies purely on 'Derm-Validated' authority, while Amazon is experimenting with a softer 'Hybrid Science' that blends clinical and natural cues.
truth_4.evidence: [PURPOSE] WWW: Authority is 'Derm-Validated (White coat authority)' vs. PDP: Authority is 'Hybrid Science'
truth_4.why_matters: This high alignment is fragile. If the 'Hybrid Science' narrative on Amazon grows stronger, it will directly contradict and undermine the core 'Derm-Validated' authority that the brand is built on, creating a new, more dangerous gap.
truth_4.the_hard_choice: Your authority is consistent for now, but the foundation is cracking. Your Amazon team is testing a new source of truth. You must decide: either enforce the strict 'Derm-Validated' narrative on Amazon to maintain absolute consistency (Option A), or recognize this is a market signal and officially evolve the brand's purpose to embrace 'Hybrid Science' everywhere (Option B). Authority must be absolute. The moment it has two sources, it has none.

truth_5.headline: Your Personality Is Bolder On Amazon
truth_5.gap_description: Your core identity as 'The Guardian' (Caregiver/Everyman/Sage) is perfectly consistent. However, the Amazon PDP executes this personality with far more confidence, wit, and humanity. The marketplace channel is outperforming your brand home in bringing the strategy to life.
truth_5.evidence: [PERSONALITY] WWW: 'We care about all women, female-identifying and non-binary people.' (Formal) vs. PDP: 'Get out of here! Banish bacteria from your scared skingdom...' (Playful)
truth_5.why_matters: The perfect alignment proves the strategy is sound, but the bolder execution on Amazon proves your own website is too conservative. You are leaving engagement and personality on the table on your most controlled channel, making your brand home feel boring.
truth_5.the_hard_choice: Your Amazon copywriter is outperforming your brand team. The data shows perfect strategic alignment but a massive gap in creative courage. Option A: Rein in the Amazon copy to match the safer, more corporate tone of the website. Option B: Acknowledge that the witty, human voice on Amazon is the future of the brand and rewrite the website to match its energy. A perfect strategy is useless without a personality to deliver it. Amazon has found your voice; your website has not.

marker_1.so_what: A highly consistent purpose, but a subtle drift in the source of authority (Derm vs. Hybrid) on PDP is a risk to monitor.

marker_2.so_what: A critical disconnect. The brand is a global movement on its website but just a good product on Amazon, diluting its purpose.

marker_3.so_what: The core value of 'Safety First' is well-aligned, providing a stable foundation for brand trust across channels.

marker_4.so_what: A major gap. The brand is a 'Medical Authority' on its site but a 'Gentle Giant' on Amazon, confusing its place in the market.

marker_5.so_what: Largely consistent as a 'Trusted Friend,' but the Amazon PDP uses a much more informal, peer-to-peer authority, which could be a strength or a risk.

marker_6.so_what: Perfectly aligned. The brand knows exactly who it is (The Guardian), providing a powerful and consistent identity core.

marker_7.so_what: Both channels use a 'Gentle Guide' tone, but Amazon is more informal and conversational, suggesting an evolution of the brand voice.

marker_8.so_what: Visually rock-solid. The 'Pharma-Code' aesthetic is executed perfectly on both channels, ensuring instant recognition.

marker_9.so_what: Perfectly consistent. The 'Problem Solver' messaging framework is used effectively on both platforms.

marker_10.so_what: The 'Lab Breakthrough' story is told consistently, grounding the brand in science and innovation on both channels.

marker_11.so_what: Rock-solid alignment. 'Derm-Validation' is the primary proof point everywhere, creating a clear and consistent reason to believe.

marker_12.so_what: The USP as a 'Tech Engineer' (via Moisture Renew Blend) is consistent, clearly communicating the product's unique advantage.

marker_13.so_what: A severe disconnect. The website is a 'Hard Product Sell' while Amazon is 'Science Education,' creating a confusing user journey.

marker_14.so_what: A critical conflict in the core job-to-be-done. The product 'Restores' on the website but 'Protects' on Amazon, creating benefit confusion.

marker_15.so_what: Perfectly aligned. Both channels consistently deliver an emotional benefit of 'Relief / Safety,' a core pillar of the brand.

marker_16.so_what: The brand consistently signals 'The Pragmatist,' reinforcing its image as a smart, no-nonsense choice for consumers.

marker_17.so_what: A strong alignment on being 'The Safe Choice,' leveraging mass popularity and expert approval as social proof.
